name: Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure Act of 2022
description: US federal law (enacted as Division Y of Pub. L. 117-103, the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2022) requiring critical-infrastructure entities to report covered cyber incidents to CISA within 72 hours and ransomware payments within 24 hours. The implementing rule (Notice of Proposed Rulemaking) was published 2024-04-04 at 6 CFR Part 226.
relationToCloudSecurity: Cloud service providers serving covered critical-infrastructure entities, and covered entities operating cloud workloads, are within scope.
relationToAISecurity: AI-related incidents on critical-infrastructure systems within scope.

license.notes: Work of the US government is in the public domain in the United States per 17 USC § 105.

lifecycle.notes: Statute enacted 2022-03-15 (Pub. L. 117-103). CISA Notice of Proposed Rulemaking 2024-04-04 (Docket CISA-2022-0010, RIN 1670-AA04). Final rule pending; reporting requirements not yet in force pending implementing regs.

current_extraction.notes: Federal Register NPRM XML acquired (circia-nprm.xml). Structured per-section parsing TODO — the FR XML uses <PRORULE>/<PREAMB>/<SUPLINF>/<SECTION> structure; the statute itself is embedded inside the much larger PL 117-103 (~1000 pages of unrelated appropriations text), so we use the FR NPRM as the practical source instead.

notes: Federal Register NPRM (RIN 1670-AA04) is the practical source. PL 117-103 PDF was attempted but the relevant CIRCIA provisions occupy only ~10 pages of a 1,000+ page appropriations act.
